parameters.jsCode:
// Prompt source-of-truth: prompts/news-curator.md — edit there, paste here, save workflow.
var article = $json;
var prompt = "You are a news curator for a Ukrainian programmer interested in:\n" +
  "- Ukraine news, Russia-Ukraine war, Ukrainian politics and society\n" +
  "- World politics, international conflicts, wars, geography\n" +
  "- Software engineering, programming, developer tools, open source\n" +
  "- Artificial intelligence and machine learning\n" +
  "- Speedcubing (Rubik cube competitions and world records)\n" +
  "- Running, marathons, endurance sports\n" +
  "- Sports news (football, athletics, etc.)\n" +
  "- Science, research, discoveries\n" +
  "- Space, NASA, SpaceX, astronomy, exoplanets\n" +
  "- Trending / viral stories — what regular people are talking about (so the user stays in the loop). Score these 7-8 if they are truly viral or culturally significant, 5-6 if mildly interesting, lower if niche or boring.\n\n" +
  "Rate this article 1-10 for relevance to this person.\n" +
  "Be strict: 8-10 for truly important/exciting news, 6-7 for interesting, 1-5 for unrelated.\n" +
  "Aim for topical balance — do not under-score space/science/trending just because they are not Ukraine or AI.\n\n" +
  "Source feed hint: " + (article.feedTopic || "unknown") + "\n" +
  "Title: " + article.title + "\n" +
  "Description: " + (article.description || "").substring(0, 500) + "\n\n" +
  "Respond ONLY with valid JSON (no extra text, no markdown):\n" +
  "{\"score\": <1-10>, \"summary\": \"<exactly 2 English sentences>\", \"topics\": [\"<tag>\", ...]}\n\n" +
  "Valid topic tags: ukraine, war, politics, tech, ai, programming, speedcubing, running, sports, geography, world, science, space, trending";

return [{
  json: {
    title: article.title,
    url: article.url,
    description: article.description,
    source: article.source,
    feedTopic: article.feedTopic,
    published_at: article.published_at,
    prompt: prompt
  }
}];

parameters.body: ={{ { title: $json.title, url: $json.url, source: $json.source, published_at: $json.published_at, summary: $json.summary, score: $json.score, topics: $json.topics } }}
